result.name: Animal Science B.S.
result.templateAssets:
[{"type":"RequirementGroup","hideSectionLetters":false,"sections":[{"type":"Section","rows":[{"position":0,"cells":[{"type":"Course","course":{"courseIdentifierParentId":354189,"courseTitle":"Introductory Companion Animal Biology","courseNumber":"042","prefix":"ANS","prefixParentId":2349,"prefixDescription":"Animal Science","departmentParentId":665,"department":"Animal Science","begin":"F2022","end":"","minUnits":4.0,"maxUnits":4.0,"pathways":[]},"visibleCrossListedCourses":[],"requisites":[],"courseAttributes":[],"id":"4f78fa2c-2428-4fce-43d3-08ddb3499771","position":0,"attributes":[]}],"attributes":[]}],"advisements":[],"position":0,"attributes":[]}],"attributes":[],"showConjunctionBetweenSections":false,"academicYearId":0,"institutionParentId":0,"instruction":{"type":"Following","id":"c488e9e9-4368-483c-7f29-08ddb3499762","selectionType":"Complete"},"advisements":[],"groupId":"fe287745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":15,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"Career Alternatives","groupId":"0c297745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

A wide range of career opportunities are available to graduates. The primary goal of the major is to prepare students for graduate study leading to the M.S. and Ph.D. degrees; for continued study in a professional school such as veterinary medicine, human medicine, or dentistry; for careers in research, agricultural production, farm and ranch management, or positions in business, sales, financial services, health care, agricultural extension, consulting services, teaching, journalism, or laboratory technology.

","groupId":"12297745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"Admissions Information","groupId":"15297745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG)","groupId":"1b297745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":6,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"CHEMISTRY","groupId":"57f67845-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":5,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"BIOLOGICAL SCIENCE","groupId":"fecc7645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"MATHEMATICS","groupId":"88d77645-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":8,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

The Animal Science major is devoted to the sciences central to understanding biological function of domestic and captive animals, their care, management, and utilization by people for food, fiber, companionship, work, and recreation. Advances in science and technology, and an ever-growing human population, have increased the complexity of issues surrounding the care and management of animals. Specializations within the major allow students to develop a scientific appreciation of animals and their relationship to their environment. Graduates in Animal Science are able to advance the science and technology of animal care and management in an objective and effective manner for the betterment of animals and society.


The curriculum provides depth in the biological and physiological sciences and allows students to specialize within the broad field of applied animal science. Study begins with introductory courses in animal science, biology, chemistry, mathematics, and statistics. Students undertake advanced courses in animal behavior, biochemistry, genetics, nutrition, and physiology and the integration of these sciences to animal growth, production, and performance. Students complete the curriculum by choosing a specialization in either an animal science discipline (behavior, biochemistry, genetics, nutrition, or physiology) or in the sciences particular to a class of animals (aquatic, avian, companion and captive, equine, laboratory, livestock and dairy, or poultry).

SCIENCE","groupId":"72207945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

For the current academic year, this major is not currently screening for major preparation at the time of admission; however, it is strongly encouraged that you complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer. Incomplete foundational coursework may delay your ability to graduate on time.


Important note: The Animal Science B.S. major will become a Selective Major beginning fall, 2027


This will be a competitive major and subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.


Important Information:

Complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series:

○    Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C

○    Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C

○    Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B or 021A/B


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

At the time of TAG submission, this major requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA, with no grade lower than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee. Fall 27 applicants: at the time of TAG submission, Animal Science requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
